description.task: Create a DataStructureClassifier class that categorizes data structures into Linear (Array, LinkedList, Stack, Queue) and Non-Linear (Tree, Graph) types. Implement methods to classify a given name and list all structures of a given category.
instructions: Populate the static map with at least 4 linear structures: Array, LinkedList, Stack, Queue
Populate with at least 2 non-linear structures: Tree, Graph
classify() performs a case-insensitive lookup; return 'unknown' if not found
getAllOfType() returns all names whose value equals the given type string
description.hint: Store all keys in lowercase in the map. In classify(), do classifications.getOrDefault(name.toLowerCase(), 'unknown'). For getAllOfType(), stream entries filtering by value equalsIgnoreCase.

description.task: Implement a DataStructureAdvisor class that recommends the best data structure given a set of requirements. Requirements include: ordered insertion, fast lookup, LIFO access, FIFO access, key-value storage, and hierarchical relationships.
instructions: recommend() checks requirements in the predefined priority order shown in the skeleton
justification() returns a map with at least 6 entries (Stack, Queue, HashMap, Tree, HashSet, ArrayList)
Each justification is a non-empty string describing why that structure is useful
The recommend() method logic is provided — focus on implementing justification()
Justification map keys must exactly match the names returned by recommend()
description.hint: In justification(), use Map.of() or put() to add entries. Example: put('Stack', 'Ideal for LIFO operations like undo/redo and function call stacks').

description.task: Build a MemoryLayoutVisualizer class that shows how data structures differ in memory usage. Given a list of elements, compare the logical memory layout of a contiguous structure (array) vs a node-based structure (linked list) in terms of wasted vs overhead bytes.
instructions: comparison() is already implemented — focus on the three calculation methods
All calculations must use the named constants (INT_SIZE, POINTER_SIZE, NODE_OVERHEAD)
description.hint: linkedListMemoryBytes: each node costs NODE_OVERHEAD + INT_SIZE + POINTER_SIZE = 16+4+8 = 28 bytes. So total = elementCount * 28.
description.expectedOutput:
Elements: 10 | Array: 56 bytes | LinkedList: 280 bytes | Ratio: 5.00x
Elements: 100 | Array: 416 bytes | LinkedList: 2800 bytes | Ratio: 6.73x
LL overhead per element: 224 bytes
